title: Sunset
lyrics: Even your sunset makes me think of life All the pink, orange, purple colors, no one about them And no one on the dime, as pretty in the end There's no need to fuss, yeah, baby It's just the sunset of the ideal The thing that you wanted to roam through your life and get into Have a little bit of the life to come back to you It's the sunset of that, you know When you go back, don't sit with Greg Don't sit, smoke that instead of what you want to Hey, just remember that you did this to you It's the sunset with pink, purple crests, haze, and toes and energies Of frozen lives that foundationally is just lost inside Crying all night over something that was so good Remorse you don't have to ever look back Don't, I don't ever need that I don't need to know that there was a chance that maybe you could have seen it in a different clue But you're just afraid Of all the colors that come along with being you Yeah, you're just afraid of what it means To never acknowledge your own hurt Than to anyone else, actually, that you can hurt Of course not, pride, pride is too much for that And you can't do it, and you won't It doesn't mean I have to sit here and be taken Taken for granted by anything that I've done I looked at it like a lollabillity, lollabillity, lollaloopsie, whoops, whoops, whoopsie-doo I ain't arguing over spilled milk Thought you trusted it in turn, and then you didn't, you don't want it, you don't It's hard for you to say, but I'll say it for you It's the sunset when it looks pretty Cause even the end of the thing that you thought was for you, it's obviously not So stop crying over the fact that you've gotta be me And get it over with so I can start Remembering that I didn't want to be gone And I tried my best to hold on And I shouldn't punish myself anymore So, look at that sunset with its red, yellow, blue Look at that view of what to be was something good But as it goes down in time, the society's lines and your own mistakes made it so you can't have me here And you gotta make your own change, and you made that But there's a special place in somewhere or in the places afterwards for people like you What do you do with all the ghosts and all the bodies you fucking hid on your ride home? Cause you could've had the hope of that, but you don't

lyricsSegments.text: Even your sunset makes me think of life All the pink, orange, purple colors, no one
about them And no one on the dime, as pretty in the end
There's no need to fuss, yeah, baby It's just the sunset of the ideal
The thing that you wanted to roam through your life and get into
Have a little bit of the life to come back to you
It's the sunset of that, you know
When you go back, don't sit with Greg Don't sit, smoke that instead of what you want to
Hey, just remember that you did this to you
It's the sunset with pink, purple crests, haze, and toes and energies
Of frozen lives that foundationally is just lost inside
Crying all night over something that was so good
Remorse you don't have to ever look back Don't, I don't ever need that
I don't need to know that there was a chance that maybe you could have seen it in a different clue
But you're just afraid
Of all the colors that come along with being you
Yeah, you're just afraid of what it means
To never acknowledge your own hurt
Than to anyone else, actually, that you can hurt
Of course not, pride, pride is too much for that
And you can't do it, and you won't
It doesn't mean I have to sit here and be taken
Taken for granted by anything that I've done
I looked at it like a lollabillity, lollabillity, lollaloopsie, whoops, whoops, whoopsie-doo
I ain't arguing over spilled milk
Thought you trusted it in turn, and then you didn't, you don't want it, you don't
It's hard for you to say, but I'll say it for you
It's the sunset when it looks pretty
Cause even the end of the thing that you thought was for you, it's obviously not
So stop crying over the fact that you've gotta be me
And get it over with so I can start
Remembering that I didn't want to be gone
And I tried my best to hold on
And I shouldn't punish myself anymore
So, look at that sunset with its red, yellow, blue
Look at that view of what to be was something good
But as it goes down in time, the society's lines and your own mistakes made it so you can't have me here
And you gotta make your own change, and you made that
But there's a special place in somewhere or in the places afterwards for people like you
What do you do with all the ghosts and all the bodies you fucking hid on your ride home?
Cause you could've had the hope of that, but you don't
